description.text:
Bumps [electron](https://github.com/electron/electron) from 38.3.0 to 38.4.0.
Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v38.4.0

Release Notes for v38.4.0

Features

  • Support dynamic ESM imports in non-context isolated preloads. #48489 (Also in 39)

Fixes

  • Fixed shader-f16 to work on Windows. #48555 (Also in 37, 39)
  • Fixed white flash on call to BrowserWindow.show. #48560 (Also in 37, 39)
  • Titlebar buttons now appear on the correct side of windows on Wayland in Ubuntu. #48602 (Also in 39)
Commits
  • 5e29e21 fix: position window titlebar buttons correctly in Ubuntu on Wayland (#48602)
  • d0db2ec feat: dynamic ESM import in preload without context isolation (#48489)
  • 4a2f733 build: use one build target (#48527) (#48605)
  • be4805a fix: fixed white flash on call to BrowserWindow.show (#48560)
  • 41721fc fix: enable shader-f16 on windows (#48555)
  • See full diff in compare view

summary: Tag Autocomplete Inconsistency Across Entry Points

description.text:
### Description

It is possible to create nested tags in YAML frontmatter via
```
---
tags: topic/economics
---
```
These tags will then autocomplete when the first part of the tag is typed in, and then the second part is navigated to with arrow keys. The tag will not autocomplete if you enter the slash, as it believes the tag is complete. The tag can be manually finished and remains _almost_ fully functional in that it is searchable, but it does not show up in tag cloud. In addition the coloring is different on the second part of the tag, confirming that the parser doesn't recognize the full tag.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d3c4165-da0d-42c5-8daa-4a3919d41bd6)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8cc4117e-a5d1-4d60-8196-9350d5034d1c)

### Reproducing

Probably not needed for this.Let me know if you'd like more information.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

Flathub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

- [ ] macOS

summary: Links made in first header not populating graph or backlinks.

description.text:
### Description

In a peculiar turn, none of the internal inks between files are functioning as they should (beyond being clickable links). This means that, as most of my notes consist solely of a title, there are only one or two connections on my graph. This also means that notes which are connected don't show up in "related".

### Reproducing


1. Create a note.
2. Create a header like; # One must beware the [[20251024174808|fallacy of compositon]] when switching from [[20251024162651|microeconomics]] to [[20251024162701|macroeconomics]].
3. Open graph view.
4. None of the notes are connected.
5. Open Related
6. None of the notes are related
7. Click or over on link
8. Link is connected

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

Flathub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Zorin OS LTS

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Markdown badge links are not rendered correctly

description.text:
### Description

First of all, thank you for your work on Zettlr — it’s a great tool for writing in Markdown.

I noticed that badge images display correctly, but **adding a link around the badge breaks the rendering**.
Here’s a minimal example to reproduce the issue:

```markdown
<!-- ✅ Works -->
![Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-13/10/2025-darkgreen?)

<!-- ❌ Doesn't work -->
[![Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-13/10/2025-darkgreen?)](https://pandoc.org/)
```

Expected behavior:
The second example should render as a clickable badge that links to `https://pandoc.org/`.

Actual behavior:
The badge is not rendered correctly when wrapped in a link.

### Reproducing

**Steps to reproduce:**

1. Open a Markdown file in Zettlr.
2. Paste the code above.

```markdown
[![Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-13/10/2025-darkgreen?)](https://pandoc.org/)
```
3. View the preview.



### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Ubuntu 22.04

### Additional Information

_No response_

  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR mainly refactors the footnote cleanup handling so that it is debounced.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The footnote cleanup handling was moved to a `ViewPlugin` so that it can be debounced after some delay in typing. This improves user typing performance. 

The logic was also refactored to address some bugs related to footnote removal. A notable change is in how footnotes are sorted -- previously, they were concated into one long string and inserted into the region contained by the first and last footnote. However, this causes issues when text occurs between footnotes, where the in-between text would be removed. ~~The new handling tries to avoid this kind of destructive action, but this could be further tested.~~ All footnote references are now moved to the end of the document.

~~The citation handler was updated so that it uses a `Set` when searching for valid locator labels. This is a much faster approach than using the `Array.find()`.~~

~~The footnote parser regex was updated to use character exclusion rather than non-greedy matching, which is much slower in general.~~

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
I don't use footnotes in my day-to-day, so my testing is quite limited to the dev environment files. I would appreciate others testing this to catch any other bugs!

In addition, I think it would be good to add a toggle for this behavior in the settings. I could see usecases where people do not want Zettlr automatically updated references.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

  longer.

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR implements a debounced word counting plugin to improve typing performance.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The word count computation was moved to a `ViewPlugin` so that it can be debounced using `setTimeout()`. It uses a `StateEffect` to update the existing `countField` value.

Event listeners were added for the new `updateWordCountEffect` so that the UI shows updated counts when they arrive rather than waiting for cursor activity.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
Currently, word counts are recomputed every keypress. In larger documents (>50,000 words) this starts to add a small but noticeable, and increasing, delay. By debouncing the counting, typing performance is dramatically improved in large documents. 

Word counts are no longer live, however, they are updated the moment the transaction is dispatched.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version -->

summary: Manually deleting a footnote deletes all subsequent footnotes

description.text:
### Description

When a user presses backspace to delete a footnote character by character, after the colon is deleted, all subsequent footnotes are also removed.

### Reproducing

Open the `Miscellaneous/Footnotes Test` file and manually delete one of the footnotes.

### Zettlr version

develop

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

macOS 26

### Additional Information
The issue is especially bad the more footnotes are below the one being deleted, causing a cascade effect.

summary: Significant performance regression with the new footnote handling

description.text:
### Description

I'm experiencing some significant performance regressions with the new footnote parser, specifically the `cleanupFootnotesAndNumbering` method [here](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/blob/b4812a5669c82d09becf51c31a5b16ce782c2cca/source/common/modules/markdown-editor/commands/footnotes.ts#L199). I'll be looking into this further, but I just wanted to document my experience.

### Reproducing

Loading a document of about 100,000 words, I get 5+ second typing delays all related to the `cleanupFootnotesAndNumbering` function.

### Zettlr version

develop

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

- [ ] Linux

summary: Zettlr Crashes on Start With Symlinks in Directory Tree

description.text:
### Description

Just had a bug (on the most recent develop branch) where I added a softlink (`ln -s source target`) into the directory tree for Zettlr, and subsequently I couldn't start Zettlr. The start was aborted with an error that the app couldn't load the main window. This is spurious because it was certainly caused by the symlink (it worked again after removing the symlink), but somehow the actual error got swallowed in the boot process.

### Reproducing

Have a symlink in your loaded workspaces.

### Zettlr version

develop

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform
